title: CR-CV-214 — Request for Inclusion on Juror Source List
purpose: Allows a citizen to voluntarily request placement on the juror source list maintained by the Secretary of State for prospective juror selection.
governing_law: - **14 M.R.S. § 1252-A (juror source list)** - **17-A M.R.S. § 453 (false statements penalty)**

title: CR-CV-FM-199 — Notice of Change of Address
court: Superior Court / District Court / Unified Criminal Docket
purpose: Notifies the court of a party's change of mailing address, physical address, telephone, or email in any pending case type.
governing_law: - **19-A M.R.S. § 4008 (confidential contact information in family/PFA cases)**

title: CR-CV-FM-255 — Notice Regarding Electronic Service
purpose: This form allows self-represented parties to opt in to receiving service of documents by email instead of regular mail. Represented parties are automatically subject to electronic service under the court rules. This form is exchanged between parties, NOT filed with the court.
governing_law: - **M.R. Civ. P. 5** — Service of pleadings and papers - **M.R.U.Cr.P. 49(d)** — Electronic service in criminal cases

title: CR-CV-FM-260 — Motion for Alternative Format for Court Proceeding
court: Superior Court / District Court / Unified Criminal Docket
purpose: Requests to change the format of a scheduled court appearance (e.g., from in-person to video/telephone or vice versa).

title: CR-CV-FM-265 — Request for Remote Hearing Coordinates
court: Superior Court / District Court / Unified Criminal Docket
purpose: Requests the remote hearing connection information (Zoom/phone coordinates) for a scheduled hearing.

title: CR-CV-FM-JV-021 — Entry of Appearance
companions: None (standalone, filed in any case type)
purpose: This form enters an attorney's appearance as counsel for a party, or a self-represented party's appearance, in any Maine court case (civil, criminal, family, juvenile). Filed with the clerk and served on all other parties.
governing_law: - **M.R. Civ. P. 89** — Appearance of attorneys - **M.R.U.Cr.P. 44** — Right to and assignment of counsel

title: CR-CV-FM-JV-166 — Motion for Transcript at State Expense
court: Superior Court / District Court / Unified Criminal Docket
purpose: Requests preparation of a court transcript at state expense when the party cannot afford to pay.

title: CR-CV-FM-JV-PA-PC-286 — Motion to Continue
court: Superior Court / District Court / Unified Criminal Docket
purpose: Requests postponement (continuance) of a scheduled court event to a later date.
keywords: postpone reschedule continue continuance adjourn delay move hearing trial date court date later date
governing_law: - **M.R. Civ. P. 40 (assignment of cases)** - **M.R.U. Crim. P. 25A (continuances)**

title: CR-CV-FM-PC-200 — Social Security Number Confidential Disclosure Form
court: Superior Court / District Court / Unified Criminal Docket
purpose: Confidentially discloses Social Security numbers to the court. Required in certain case types.

title: CR-CV-JV-297 — Personal Identifying Information Attachment
purpose: Provides a separate attachment for personal identifying information (PII) that must be redacted or omitted from public court documents per M.R.E.C.S.
governing_law: - **M.R.E.C.S. 2, 4(E)(2), 5(B), 6(F)(2) (electronic court system rules on PII redaction)**

title: CR-JV-253 — Notification That Observation Is No Longer Necessary and Order Terminating Commitment
court: Unified Criminal Docket / Superior Court / District Court
purpose: This is a court order form used in criminal cases. Typically completed by the court or prepared by counsel for judicial signature.

title: CV-001 — Civil Summary Sheet
companions: Required with every civil complaint; CV-261 (Contract Case Cover Sheet) required for contract cases
purpose: The Civil Summary Sheet is required by the Clerk of Court to initiate or update the civil docket in Maine Superior or District Court. It collects case classification information including court, parties, nature of action, jury demand, and related case data. This form does not replace filing and service of pleadings.
governing_law: - **M.R. Civ. P. 3** — Commencement of action - **M.R. Civ. P. 11** — Signing and verification of pleadings - **M.R. Civ. P. 38** — Jury trial demand

title: CV-007 — Complaint for Residential Forcible Entry
companions: CV-034 (Summons, $5 from clerk), CV-100 (Instructions), CV-256 (Info Sheet & Mediation Request), CV-204 (Affidavit of Service)
purpose: This is the complaint form to begin a residential eviction (Forcible Entry and Detainer) case in Maine District Court. Filed by a landlord (plaintiff) against tenant(s) (defendant) to recover possession of residential property.
governing_law: - **M.R. Civ. P. 80D** — Forcible Entry and Detainer procedure - **14 M.R.S. §§ 6001–6016** — FED statutes (notice requirements, grounds, procedure) - § 6001: Availability of remedy - § 6002: Grounds for FED action - § 6001-A: Notice to quit requirements - § 6002-A: Additional protections for residential tenants - § 6003: Procedure and service - § 6005: Judgment and writ of possession - § 6014: Rent escrow

title: CV-037 — Subpoena for Hearing on Motion
companions: Motion for Contempt (attorney-drafted), Return of Service (page 2)
purpose: This subpoena compels a party to appear at a hearing on a Motion for Contempt filed against them. It commands appearance at a specific court date/time and may also require production of documents. Page 2 contains the Return of Service for the process server.
governing_law: - **M.R. Civ. P. 66(d)** — Contempt proceedings - **M.R. Civ. P. 45** — Subpoenas generally

title: CV-060 — Request for Withdrawal (Minor’s Settlement)
purpose: This form requests court approval to withdraw funds from a minor's settlement account. When a minor receives a settlement, funds are typically deposited in a protected account. This form is used by the next friend or petitioner to request withdrawal, which requires court approval under M.R. Civ. P. 17A(b)(5).
governing_law: - **M.R. Civ. P. 17A(b)(5)** — Minor settlements, withdrawal of funds

title: CV-061 — Affidavit and Request for Default
purpose: This form is used to request entry of default and default judgment when a defendant has failed to appear, plead, or otherwise defend a civil action. The affiant (plaintiff or plaintiff's attorney) must swear to specific facts including the defendant's failure to respond, non-military status, and the amount owed.
governing_law: - **M.R. Civ. P. 55** — Default and default judgment - **50 U.S.C. § 3911, § 3931** — Servicemembers Civil Relief Act (no default against active military) - **M.R. Civ. P. 11** — Signing and verification

title: CV-066 — Petition and Order for Military Certification
purpose: Petitions the court to issue an order to military branches to determine if a party is in active military service, when their whereabouts cannot be ascertained.

title: CV-067 — Application to Proceed Without Payment of Fees
purpose: Requests waiver of court fees (filing, service, mediation, jury, appeal) based on financial inability to pay.

title: CV-092 — How to File a Civil Appeal
purpose: Instruction document explaining how to file a civil appeal to the Maine Law Court. Not applicable to criminal, eviction, or small claims appeals.
governing_law: - **M.R. App. P. (Maine Rules of Appellate Procedure)**

title: CV-100 — Instructions for Filing a Residential FED Case
purpose: Step-by-step instructions for landlords on filing a residential Forcible Entry and Detainer (eviction) case in Maine District Court.

title: CV-145 — Report of ADR Conference and Order
purpose: This form reports the outcome of an Alternative Dispute Resolution (ADR) conference in Superior Court. It documents whether the case was unresolved, partially resolved, or fully resolved through mediation, early neutral evaluation, or arbitration, and is submitted to the court as an order.
governing_law: - **M.R. Civ. P. 16B** — Alternative Dispute Resolution - **4 M.R.S. § 18-B** — Court ADR programs

title: CV-172 — Newspaper Cover Letter (Service by Publication)
purpose: Cover letter sent to a newspaper with an order for service by publication, directing publication for three successive weeks.

title: CV-173 — Verification (of Publication)
purpose: Newspaper employee's sworn verification that service by publication was completed as ordered — published once a week for three successive weeks.

title: CV-181 — Request for (Alias/Pluries) Writ of Execution
purpose: This form requests a renewal writ of execution when the original writ has expired or been returned unsatisfied. It allows a judgment creditor to obtain a new writ to enforce a money judgment, provided certain conditions are met under 14 M.R.S. § 4651-A.
governing_law: - **14 M.R.S. § 4651-A** — Renewal writs of execution - **14 M.R.S. § 4656** — Lost or destroyed writs - **14 M.R.S. § 2018** — Void executions - **M.R. Civ. P. 11** — Signing and verification

title: CV-183 — Complaint for Recovery Personal Property
companions: CV-218 (Instructions), CV-188 (Writ of Possession), CV-187 (Notice of Appeal)
purpose: This is the complaint form to initiate a summary process action in District Court to recover personal property. The plaintiff claims a right, title, or interest in specific personal property that the defendant possesses and refuses to return, under 14 M.R.S. § 7071.
governing_law: - **14 M.R.S. § 7071** — Recovery of personal property (summary process)

title: CV-187 — Notice of Appeal – Recovery of Personal Property
purpose: This form is used to appeal a judgment in a personal property recovery (summary process) action under 14 M.R.S. § 7071(8). The appellant may appeal to the Law Court or to Superior Court for a jury trial de novo.
governing_law: - **14 M.R.S. § 7071(8)** — Appeal of personal property recovery judgment - **M.R. App. P. 5(d)** — Statement in lieu of transcript

title: CV-188 — Request for Issuance of Writ of Possession for
purpose: After obtaining a judgment in a personal property recovery action, this form requests the court to issue a Writ of Possession directing the sheriff to retrieve the personal property and deliver it to the plaintiff.
governing_law: - **14 M.R.S. § 7071(6)** — Writ of possession for personal property

title: CV-191 — Financial Affidavit
companions: Motion to Proceed Without Payment of Fees
purpose: This financial affidavit supports a request to proceed without payment of court fees (in forma pauperis) or other financial-related motions. It requires detailed disclosure of income, assets, expenses, and dependents.
governing_law: - **M.R. Civ. P. 91** — Proceedings in forma pauperis - Court fee waiver policies

title: CV-195 — Request for Issuance of Writ of Possession
purpose: After obtaining a judgment in a Forcible Entry and Detainer (eviction) case, this form requests the court to issue a Writ of Possession directing the sheriff to remove the tenant and restore possession to the landlord. Different sections apply depending on whether the judgment was based on a 30-day or 7-day Notice to Quit.
governing_law: - **14 M.R.S. § 6005** — Judgment and writ of possession in FED - **14 M.R.S. § 6002** — Grounds; reinstatement provision for rent arrearage cases

title: CV-204 — Affidavit of Service – Forcible Entry & Detainer
purpose: This affidavit documents that service of the FED summons and complaint was completed by alternative means after three unsuccessful attempts by the sheriff. It certifies that the documents were mailed and left at the defendant's abode, as required by 14 M.R.S. § 6004.
governing_law: - **14 M.R.S. § 6004** — Service in FED actions - **M.R. Civ. P. 80D** — FED procedure

title: CV-206 — Notice of Appeal – Forcible Entry and Detainer
purpose: This form is used to appeal a judgment in a Forcible Entry and Detainer (eviction) case from District Court to Superior Court. The appellant may appeal on questions of law or request a jury trial de novo. Defendants must pay current month's rent or arrearages before filing.
governing_law: - **14 M.R.S. § 6008** — Appeal in FED cases - **M.R. Civ. P. 76F(c)** — Statement in lieu of transcript

title: CV-207 — Consent for Expedited Final Hearing (Foreclosure)
purpose: Defendant's consent to scheduling an expedited final hearing in a residential foreclosure case.

title: CV-208 — Request to Schedule Expedited Final Hearing (Foreclosure)
purpose: Plaintiff's request to schedule an expedited final hearing in a residential foreclosure case after meeting statutory conditions.
governing_law: - **14 M.R.S. § 6321-B (expedited final hearing)** - **14 M.R.S. § 6321-A (mediation in foreclosure)** - **14 M.R.S. § 6322 (consent to expedited hearing)**

title: CV-218 — Instructions for Filing Summary Recovery of Personal Property Case
purpose: Instructions for filing a Summary Recovery of Personal Property case under COVID-19 procedures.

title: CV-222 — Application for Transfer to Business and Consumer Docket
purpose: Application to transfer a civil case to the Business and Consumer Docket (BCD), Maine's specialized business court.

title: CV-223 — Judicial Recommendation for Transfer to Business and Consumer Docket
purpose: Judge's recommendation to transfer a case to the Business and Consumer Docket.

title: CV-256 — Residential FED Information Sheet and Mediation Request
purpose: Information sheet for tenants facing eviction, with option to request mediation. Must be served with the Notice to Quit or complaint/summons.
keywords: tenant respond response answer reply eviction evict notice quit mediation landlord

title: CV-261 — Please check the appropriate box for the case being filed and follow instructions
purpose: Required cover sheet for all contract cases filed in Maine courts. Distinguishes between general contract actions and debt collection actions, which have different fee structures and requirements under 32 M.R.S. Chapter 109-A (Fair Debt Collection Practices).
governing_law: - **4 M.R.S. § 18-A(3-A)** — Debt collection surcharge - **32 M.R.S. § 11002** — Definitions (debt collector) - **32 M.R.S. § 11020** — Attorney debt collectors

title: CV-266 — Affidavit and Request to Register a Foreign State Money Judgment Under UEFJA
purpose: Registers a money judgment from another US state in Maine under the Uniform Enforcement of Foreign Judgments Act.

title: CV-267 — Affidavit and Request for Issuance of Subpoena Under UIDDA
purpose: Requests issuance of a Maine subpoena for use in an out-of-state case under the Uniform Interstate Depositions and Discovery Act.

title: CV-281 — Affidavit and Request to Register a Canadian Money Judgment
purpose: Registers a Canadian money judgment in Maine for enforcement.

title: CV-282 — Petition to Recognize a Foreign Country Money Judgment
purpose: Petitions Maine court to recognize a money judgment from a foreign country (non-US, non-Canadian) under the Uniform Foreign-Country Money Judgments Recognition Act.
governing_law: - **14 M.R.S. §§ 8801-8812 (Uniform Foreign-Country Money Judgments Recognition Act)**

title: CV-287 — Debt Buyer Complaint
purpose: Standardized complaint form for debt buyers filing suit to collect on purchased consumer debts. Required format ensures proper documentation of debt chain.
governing_law: - **32 M.R.S. § 11013 (debt buyer requirements)** - **Maine Fair Debt Collection Practices Act**

title: CV-295 — Petition for Special Findings and Relief (SIJS)
purpose: Petition for Special Immigrant Juvenile Status (SIJS) findings under Maine law, allowing at-risk noncitizen children to seek federal immigration relief.
governing_law: - **22 M.R.S. § 4099-I (special findings for at-risk noncitizen children)**

title: CV-CR-162 — Notice of Appeal
purpose: General-purpose Notice of Appeal form used in civil and criminal cases to appeal from a judgment, order, or ruling to a higher court. This is the standard appeal form when no case-specific appeal form exists (e.g., CV-206 for FED appeals, CV-187 for personal property appeals).
governing_law: - **M.R. App. P. 2** — Appeal as of right - **M.R. App. P. 5(d)** — Statement in lieu of transcript

title: CV-CR-163 — Request for Protection on Trial List
purpose: Requests specific dates be protected (excluded) from the trial list due to scheduling conflicts.

title: CV-CR-JV-165 — Transcript and Audio Order Form
companions: Notice of Appeal (case-specific), CV-CR-166 (Motion for Transcript at State Expense)
purpose: This form orders transcripts or audio recordings of court proceedings, typically for use in appeals. Required when filing an appeal that needs a record of the lower court proceedings. Can also be used for personal reference or use in other pending cases.
governing_law: - **M.R. App. P. 5** — Record on appeal (transcripts required for appeals)

title: CV-FM-036 — Acknowledgment of Receipt of Summons and
purpose: This form is sent with the summons and complaint (or post-judgment motion, or notice of foreign judgment registration) to the opposing party. When signed and returned, it constitutes valid service under M.R. Civ. P. 4(c)(1). If not returned within 20 days, the court may order the non-returning party to pay sheriff service costs.
governing_law: - **M.R. Civ. P. 4(c)(1)** — Service by mail with acknowledgment

title: CV-FM-072 — Motion for Service by Alternate Means and
companions: CV-FM-036 (Acknowledgment), CV-FM-202 (Affidavit of Alternate Service Completion)
purpose: When standard service methods fail (mail acknowledgment not returned, sheriff unable to serve), this form requests court permission to serve by alternate means: leaving at abode, publication in newspaper, or other means. Includes an affidavit documenting failed service attempts.
governing_law: - **M.R. Civ. P. 4(g)** — Service by alternate means

title: CV-FM-103 — Notice of Hearing
purpose: Formal notice that a hearing has been scheduled in a case, informing parties of the date, time, and location.

title: CV-FM-201 — Instructions for Service by Alternate Means
purpose: Checklist and instructions for requesting court approval to serve papers by alternate means when the other party cannot be found.

title: CV-FM-202 — Affidavit that Service was Completed by Alternate
companions: CV-FM-072 (Motion for Alternate Service), Court Order for Alternate Service
purpose: After the court grants alternate service and the party completes service by the ordered method, this affidavit documents that service was completed as directed. It is filed with the court to prove service.
governing_law: - **M.R. Civ. P. 4(g)(3)** — Proof of alternate service

title: CV-FM-203 — Request for Hearing (Foreign Order Registration)
purpose: Non-registering party's request for hearing to contest the validity or enforcement of a registered foreign state order (support, custody, or judgment).
governing_law: - **19-A M.R.S. § 3201 (UIFSA — Uniform Interstate Family Support Act)** - **19-A M.R.S. § 1765 (UCCJEA — Uniform Child Custody Jurisdiction and Enforcement Act)** - **14 M.R.S. § 8004 (UEFJA)**

title: CV-FM-270 — Motion for Appointment of Court-Paid Referee
purpose: Requests appointment of a court-paid referee to hold a settlement conference or hearing on contested issues.

title: CV-FM-271 — Consent or Objection to Appointment of Court-Paid Referee
purpose: Party's consent to or objection against appointment of a court-paid referee under JB-22-03.

title: CV-FM-273 — Notice Preserving Right to Object to Court-Paid Referee's Report
purpose: Preserves a party's right to object to a court-paid referee's report when the referee was appointed without all parties' agreement.

title: CV-FM-274 — Referee's Case Management Conference Order
purpose: Court-paid referee's order after case management conference, documenting case type, attendees, contested issues, discovery schedule, and hearing logistics.

title: CV-FM-275 — Objection to Court-Paid Referee's Report
purpose: Files specific objections to a court-paid referee's report, requesting judge review before final order.

title: CV-FM-277 — Zoom Participant List
purpose: Provides contact information to the Zoom hosting service for remote court-paid referee hearings or settlement conferences.

title: CV-FM-278 — Report of Court-Paid Referee After Settlement Conference
purpose: Referee's report after a settlement conference that did not result in agreement, recommending next steps.

purpose: Informational handout explaining what to expect when a court-paid referee has been appointed in your case.

title: CV-FM-288 — Motion for Appointment of Court-Paid Attorney Referee
purpose: Requests appointment of a specific licensed attorney as court-paid referee under JB-23-04 (attorney referee program, distinct from JB-22-03).

purpose: Party's agreement to appointment of a court-paid attorney referee under JB-23-04.

purpose: Payment voucher for court-paid attorney referees to claim hourly fees and mileage reimbursement.

title: CV-FM-PA-PB-PC-268 — Order Appointing Guardian ad Litem Under M.R. Civ. P. 17(b)

purpose: Amends an existing GAL appointment order to modify duties, duration, hours, fees, or scope.

title: FM-043 — Financial Statement

purpose: Comprehensive financial disclosure required in all Maine family cases involving property division, spousal support, or child support. Both parties must file. title: PA-037 — Sexual Assault and Domestic Violence Help
purpose: This is an informational/instruction document providing guidance for protection from abuse/harassment matters in Maine court.

purpose: This affidavit form provides sworn testimony in support of a probate matter.

purpose: Form used in probate proceedings in Maine court.

purpose: Form used in probate proceedings in Maine court.

purpose: Form used in probate proceedings in Maine court.

purpose: Form used in probate proceedings in Maine court.

purpose: Instructions for Submission of VS-9 or

title: PC-034 — Compliance Report of the
purpose: Form used in child protection proceedings in Maine court.

title: PC-043 — Motion to Extend Jeopardy Order Deadline
purpose: This motion form is used in child protection cases in Maine court. Filed to request specific court action.

title: PC-044 — Before this Court and through this written document, I hereby give my consent to the foster care placement
purpose: Form used in child protection proceedings in Maine court.

title: PUB-001 — A Guide To
purpose: This is an informational/instruction document providing guidance for publications/reference matters in Maine court.

title: SC-001 — Statement of Claim
companions: SC-005 (Acknowledgment of Service), SC-006 (Affidavit and Request for Service), SC-009/SC-010 (Instructions)
purpose: This is the initial filing form for a small claims action in Maine District Court. The plaintiff describes their claim, specifies the amount sought (up to $6,000), and provides contact information for both parties. Small claims uses simplified procedures under the Maine Rules of Small Claims Procedure.
governing_law: - **M.R.S.C.P. 3(a)** — Statement of Claim - **M.R.S.C.P. 4** — Service - **14 M.R.S. § 7482** — Small claims jurisdiction (up to $6,000)

title: SC-003 — Request for SC Disclosure Hearing
companions: SC-006 (Affidavit and Request for Service), SC-001 (original Statement of Claim)
purpose: After obtaining a small claims judgment that the debtor has not paid (30+ days), the judgment creditor uses this form to request a disclosure hearing. The debtor must appear and disclose assets and income under oath to determine ability to pay.

title: SC-005 — Acknowledgment of Service
purpose: This form is sent to the defendant along with the Statement of Claim (or Notice of Disclosure Hearing). The defendant signs and returns it to acknowledge receipt, which constitutes valid service. If not returned within 20 days, alternative service methods are needed.

title: SC-006 — Affidavit and Request for Service
purpose: This form requests the court clerk to arrange service of the Statement of Claim or Notice of Disclosure Hearing on the defendant. The plaintiff certifies they have filed fewer than three small claims this month and provides the defendant's service address.
governing_law: - **M.R.S.C.P. 4(b)** — Service by clerk - **M.R.S.C.P. 12(a)(2)** — Disclosure hearing service

title: SC-007 — Notice of Small Claims Appeal
purpose: This form is used to appeal a small claims judgment to Superior Court. Both plaintiffs and defendants may appeal, but defendants who request a jury trial de novo must also file an affidavit with specific facts showing a genuine issue of material fact.

title: SC-009 — What happens at a Small Claims hearing?
purpose: This is an informational document provided to defendants in small claims cases. It explains what happens at a hearing, how to prepare, settlement options, payment obligations if judgment is entered, and appeal rights. No fields to fill — this is served on the defendant along with the Statement of Claim.

title: SC-010 — Statement of Claim
purpose: This document provides instructions to small claims plaintiffs on how to serve the Statement of Claim on the defendant. It describes three methods of service in order of preference: mail with acknowledgment, certified/registered mail, and sheriff service.
